definition: AI aggregate productivity gains will not resolve services inflation within 2026-2028, but sectoral deployment is already live in narrow CPI-relevant categories (customer service, coding, legal review). The gap between 'AI is deployed' and 'AI is disinflationary in CPI' — since firms may capture gains as margin — is the key unresolved question.
evidence: n=3 technology analogues under heterogeneous conditions cannot support confidence 8
Cloud-based software deployment compresses adoption vs. hardware-adoption templates
Both aggregate (won't move TFP) and sectoral (already deployed) can be simultaneously true
CPI impact depends on weight of affected sub-categories, which no agent quantifies
open_questions: What is the CPI weight of AI-affected sub-categories?
Will firms pass through AI productivity gains as lower prices or capture as margin?
What is the realistic timeline for aggregate TFP impact?
Does the software vs. hardware distinction compress the adoption timeline enough to matter for 2026-2028?
